title: erodium praecox subsp. primulaceum
intro: Erodium praecox subsp. primulaceum, also known as the early herb or corn marigold, is a perennial plant native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its small, pink or white flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Erodium praecox subsp. primulaceum include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Erodium praecox subsp. primulaceum can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in folk medicine to treat various ailments.
